Description: The festival where the Lord departs for Madurai to witness the wedding of his sister, Meenakshi.
The thirty Thiruppavai hymns composed by Sri Andal are recited along with lullabies.
The birth star of Andal (Pooram) is celebrated grandly.

column9: On the 5th day, the Five Perumals appear in the Garuda Seva, and on the 9th day, the Chariot Festival (Ther) takes place.

Description: Garuda Seva on the full moon day.
Sayana festival and Mukkani (three fruits) festival.
Ten-day festival during the Swathi star. This is the place where Periyazhwar sang the "Thirupallandu".
The Navaratri festival is celebrated grandly.
A special oil abhishekam is performed using one "kottai" (a large measure) of oil. The oil is collected in a well and sold to devotees, as it is considered a medicinal cure for diseases.
Once every 6 years, one lakh (100,000) lamps are lit in the temple.

column9: This is the birthplace of Nammazhwar. Chariot festival and Theerthavari are held on his birth star.

Description: The deities of the 9 nearby Divya Desams arrive on Garuda mounts to visit Azhwar Thirunagari.
Ten-day night festival. On the 10th day, it is believed that Nammazhwar attains Moksha.

column9: The Lord provides darsan through the Paramapada Vaasal (gateway to heaven) adorned in the Ratna Angi (gem-studded armor).
The Kal Garuda (Stone Garuda) vehicle service on the 4th day of Brahmotsavam. Garuda is adorned with eight snakes as ornaments.

Description: The Kal Garuda vehicle service on the 4th day of Brahmotsavam.
When Jupiter enters the Aquarius (Kumba) zodiac sign, Kumbh Mela is celebrated.

column9: Sri Varadharaja Perumal appears on the Garuda Vahana (mount) on the 3rd day of Brahmotsavam.

The deities of the 11 Divya Desams assemble at a pandal in front of Manimada Kovil. Thirumangai Alvar performs Mangalasasanam for each deity. The 11 Divya Desam deities then process through the streets on Garuda Vahana.
